value.Title: April 23, 2026: Zachry Group Expands Footprint with New Lease near La Villita

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGJ-7N93-RRS1-C3JT-00000-00April 23, 2026: Zachry Group Expands Footprint with New Lease near La Villita2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 23, 2026: Zachry Group Expands Footprint with New Lease near La Villita

Zachry Group has recently taken over a ground lease for a parking lot near La Villita and Hemisfair, marking a strategic move to support its operations in the area. On the 29th of April 2026, the company, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, finalized the lease agreement with St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Church, acquiring the rights to a parking lot situated across South Presa Street from the church.

In addition to the lease, Zachry Group also purchased a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the total area acquired to approximately 1.2 acres. Both properties were previously associated with Dennis McDaniel, an Austin developer who had intended to build an apartment complex on the site before the onset of the pandemic disrupted those plans.

According to a representative from Zachry Group, the company intends to utilize the parking lots for operational purposes related to the luxury Monarch San Antonio hotel, which recently opened its doors overlooking Civic Park. There are currently no plans for further development on the newly acquired properties, indicating a focus on supporting existing hospitality operations rather than pursuing new construction projects.

The properties were originally part of a larger development plan initiated by McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio, the city's public housing authority. This ambitious project, which was to feature 252 apartments and retail space, fell through as Opportunity Home stepped back during the pandemic, leaving the construction plans unfulfilled.

Zachry Group, a prominent player in various sectors including construction and hospitality, has a long-standing history in San Antonio. Founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, the company has contributed significantly to local developments, including the recent $185 million Monarch hotel project. With its roots deeply entrenched in the city’s growth, Zachry Group continues to play a vital role in shaping San Antonio's urban landscape.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

Mission: Excellence in Service

Zachry Group's mission is clear: "To be the best at what we do, delivering distinctive value to our customers." This unwavering commitment drives their pursuit of operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

Vision: The Trusted Leader

The company's vision reflects its aspirations: "To be the most trusted name in industrial infrastructure services." This trust is built on a foundation of consistent performance, integrity, and innovation.

Core Values: Principles That Drive Success

Zachry Group's core values define its culture and approach to business:

Think Long Term: Decisions are made with future generations in mind.

Care About People: The safety, health, and well-being of employees are paramount.

Do the Right Thing: Integrity, honesty, and courage guide all actions.

Honor Commitments: Reliability and trustworthiness are central to their operations.

The Power of "We": Collaboration and teamwork drive success.

Make a Positive Impact: Leaving every project and community better than before.

By adhering to these values, the Zachry Group continues to shape North America's industrial landscape with innovation, trust, and a long-term perspective.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.zachrygroup.com/

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

Zachry Group was ranked 127 in the Forbes list of Top 216 American Private Companies by Total Revenues in 2024.

value.Title: May 01, 2026: - CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY: ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGN-S093-S3VB-54MY-00000-00May 01, 2026: - CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY: ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 01, 2026: - CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY: ANALYZING LATEST PEER GROUP NEWS

Clayco Construction Company is expanding its footprint in the renewable energy sector with the launch of a new business unit focused on large solar energy and storage projects across the United States. Announced on March 4, 2026, the initiative establishes Clayco Power and Energy, which aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate an anticipated annual revenue of approximately $300 million. The unit provides design, engineering, construction, and procurement services for solar and battery storage systems, starting with a 350-acre development in Manteno, Illinois. Clayco CEO Anthony Johnson emphasized the importance of this expansion, stating, "Solar must be part of the solution. It is the fastest type of power to bring to the market and should work alongside fossil fuels, natural gas, and nuclear energy."

In a separate technological advancement announced on February 12, 2026, Clayco revealed a collaboration with Swiss American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for the first nuclear-powered AI data center campus in the United States. The proposed facility, targeted for the Idaho National Laboratory, will utilize Clayco's early-stage delivery planning and integrated design-build expertise. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, highlighted the need for confidence in project delivery for successful government submissions. William G. J. Theron, founder and CEO of Deep Atomic, noted that the partnership aims to accelerate the deployment of advanced nuclear power solutions to meet the sustainability needs of next-generation computing.

Shifting to peer industry movements, Ryan Companies announced the strategic acquisition of Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm Meritax Advisors on February 10, 2026. The integration aims to enhance Ryan's property tax capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management, and large-portfolio advisory services. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, "With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we're adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape." In conjunction with the acquisition, key Meritax partners and 20 additional team members will join Ryan Companies, expanding its footprint in business taxes.

Additionally, Ryan Companies recently highlighted the leadership of its director of construction, Anna Griffin, who shared military-inspired leadership insights on a regional business podcast recorded a few days ago. Transitioning from her service in the Air Force, Griffin manages construction projects across the Southwest for Ryan Companies. Reflecting on her career, she stated, "Construction has so many parallels to my military service. You're still serving — just in a different way. You're building something lasting, something that supports people and communities."

value.Title: Ellomay Capital Announces the Filing of the Annual Report on Form 20-F for 2025

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-BBG3-RS3P-Y1TT-00000-00Ellomay Capital Announces the Filing of the Annual Report on Form 20-F for 20252026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisPRESS RELEASESCOMPANY ANNOUNCEMENTEllomay Capital Announces the Filing of the Annual Report on Form 20-F for 2025

Tel-Aviv, Israel, , May 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Ellomay Capital Ltd.(NYSE American; TASE: ELLO) (“Ellomay” or the “Company”), a renewable energy and power generator and developer of renewable energy and power projects in Europe, USA and Israel, today announced the filing of its Annual Report on Form 20-F for the year ended December 31, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

A copy of the Annual Report on Form 20-F is available to be viewed and downloaded from the Investor Relations section of the Company’s website at http://www.ellomay.com. The Company will provide a hard copy of the Annual Report on Form 20-F, including the Company’s complete audited financial statements, free of charge to its shareholders upon request.

The financial statements included in the Annual Report on Form 20-F present certain changes compared to the unaudited financial results for the year ended and as of December 31, 2025 published by the Company on March 31, 2026. These changes include a decrease of approximately €1.5 million in project development costs mainly in connection with a reversal of provision due to a change in circumstances since the publication of the financial results, and a decrease of approximately €1.9 million in tax benefit for the year ended December 31, 2025.

About Ellomay Capital Ltd.Ellomay is an Israeli based company whose shares are registered with the NYSE American and with the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange under the trading symbol “ELLO”. Since 2009, Ellomay focuses its business in the renewable energy and power sectors in Europe, USA and Israel.To date, Ellomay has evaluated numerous opportunities and invested significant funds in the renewable, clean energy and natural resources industries in Israel, Italy, Spain, the Netherlands and Texas, USA, including:

Approximately 335.9 MW of operating solar power plants in Spain (including a 300 MW solar plant in owned by Talasol, which is 51% owned by the Company) and 51% of approximately 38 MW of operating solar power plants in Italy;

16.875% indirect interest in Dorad Energy Ltd., which owns and operates one of Israel’s largest private power plants with production capacity of approximately 850 MW;

Groen Gas Goor B.V., Groen Gas Oude-Tonge B.V. and Groen Gas Gelderland B.V., project companies operating anaerobic digestion plants in the Netherlands, with a green gas production capacity of approximately 3 million, 3.8 million and 9.5 million Nm3 per year, respectively;

83.333% of Ellomay Pumped Storage (2014) Ltd., which is involved in a project to construct a 156 MW pumped storage hydro power plant in the Manara Cliff, Israel;

51% of solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 160 MW that are under construction;

Solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 210 MW that have reached “ready to build” status; and

Solar projects in the Dallas Metropolitan area, Texas, USA with an aggregate capacity of approximately 38 MW that are connected to the grid, 11 MW that are currently in the test run phase prior to commercial operation and 14 MW that are under construction.

For more information about Ellomay, visit http://www.ellomay.com.

Contact: Kalia Rubenbach (Weintraub) CFO Tel: +972 (3) 797-1111 Email: kaliaw@ellomay.com

value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31CK-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES PROMOTION OF TRAVIS MROSS TO PRESIDENT

Zachry Construction Corporation named Travis Mross its new president, effective immediately, in a move to support growth in key infrastructure markets.Mross began with Zachry Construction in 2000 as an intern and has progressed to executive vice president, a role he has held for six years.The promotion, announced after a milestone on 21st April 2026, positions him to shape the company’s strategic direction and strengthen operating capabilities across its core markets.He will emphasize growth and operational excellence across transportation, water, underground utilities, and civil sitework, while strengthening the company culture and maintaining a disciplined, results-oriented approach to large-scale projects.The leadership change comes as Zachry expands through the Crescent Constructors acquisition, boosting capabilities in water and wastewater plant infrastructure to meet rising demand. With more than a century of experience, the company remains focused on safety, technical proficiency, and reliable project management as it pursues future opportunities.

+ ZACHRY GROUP REBRANDS IN STRATEGIC PUSH INTO DATA CENTERS AND WATER SECTOR

Zachry Construction Corp. unveiled a refreshed brand identity, including a new logo and the tagline 'Unearth Opportunity,' as it positions itself for growth in data-center and water-infrastructure markets. The San Antonio-based company announced the change on Feb. 4, 2026, saying the branding update aligns with its South Texas heritage while signaling an expanded market focus.Executive Vice President Travis Mross said the rebrand reflects the firm’s evolution and clarity of purpose for clients and stakeholders. He noted the company will provide data-center sitework and underground utilities—from excavation to drainage—highlighting its role as a partner in building critical digital infrastructure.The data-center push is complemented by a broader foray into water infrastructure. In October, Zachry completed the Crescent Constructors Inc. acquisition, a move into the process and treatment side of water projects. Mross described the water sector as among the fastest growing in construction and said Zachry intends to capitalize on that momentum.Zachry’s track record spans both public and private work, with more than 5,500 projects completed. The rebrand aims to improve communication of the company’s mission to clients and the public, with Mross asserting that 'Just like the industry evolves, society evolves, so does our logo.' The company traces its origins to H.B. Zachry Co., founded in 1924, and after a 2008 reorganization, Zachry Construction and Zachry Group became two distinct entities that continue to shape the industry today.

+ ZACHRY GROUP NAMED LEAD FOR FEED ON GROUNDBREAKING LOW-CARBON METHANOL INITIATIVE IN LAKE CHARLES

Zachry Group has been named to lead the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for a pioneering low-carbon blue methanol project in Lake Charles, Louisiana. Six days earlier, the firm disclosed its selection, underscoring its role in advancing sustainable industrial development. The plant targets capturing about 1 million tpy of CO2, setting a benchmark for methanol production and aiding decarbonization efforts across the industrial sector. The FEED phase will establish the project's technical and economic foundations, paving the way toward a final investment decision (FID) anticipated in the second quarter of 2026. John Zachry, Chairman and CEO, commented: "We are honoured to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project. This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale." His remarks emphasize the company’s commitment to sustainable and innovative industrial practices. As the energy transition toward decarbonization continues, Zachry Group's participation highlights its strategic focus on low-carbon technologies. The project is designed to contribute significantly to reducing greenhouse gas emissions across the industrial landscape. Investors and industry stakeholders are watching progress closely, as successful FEED could set a precedent for future low-carbon ventures. With an FID expected in early 2026, Zachry Group could occupy a pivotal role in the evolving landscape of sustainable energy solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ UNC CHARLOTTE LAUNCHES ALBERT SCHOOL OF CONSTRUCTION WITH MAJOR BECHTEL GIFT

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have given a leadership gift to establish the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering, launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation’s fastest-growing markets. The announcement was made Apr. 7, 2026. The school will focus on the full capital project lifecycle, training students to lead from planning and engineering through delivery of complex infrastructure. Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber noted that the investment helps align education with regional and national needs. Bechtel Group’s Craig Albert underscored the industry’s demand for skilled workers, citing a real shortage of construction capacity.

+ LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD BECHTEL’S MINING & METALS AS AILIE MACADAM RETIRES

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam prepares to retire in May after a 41-year Bechtel career. Craig Albert, Bechtel President and COO, stated on 3rd April 2026, "Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision, deep mining sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus to advance our Mining & Metals business." Martin has spent over two decades at Bechtel, where she has developed high-performing global teams aimed at solving complex challenges for customers across various markets. In her new role, Martin said she intends to build on Bechtel's momentum in mining, acknowledging a rapidly evolving global market. "Our focus will be on enhancing how we deliver results for our customers, particularly in areas such as cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes," she added. MacAdam's tenure delivered major project progress in key markets and led to the highest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals in 2025. Bechtel said it remains a trusted partner across sectors including Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and will continue to address the demands of a changing energy landscape under its new leader.

+ BECHTEL SECURES MASTER-PLANNING ROLE FOR HENDERSON NAVAL HUB

The Australian government has authorised Bechtel Infrastructure Australia to lead the master-planning design services for the Henderson Defence Precinct, aimed at strengthening domestic shipbuilding and sustainment capacity in Western Australia. The confirmation came on 9 March 2026, marking a pivotal step in the nation’s defence program.Funding for the project has progressed in stages, with $127 million earmarked in 2024 to advance planning, and a further multi-billion-dollar commitment last year to support delivery. Bechtel’s participation is expected to be instrumental in realising these commitments and is aligned with plans to sustain thousands of direct jobs in the region over the next twenty years.The precinct will accommodate a range of critical assets, including facilities for landing craft, the Mogami-class frigates, conventional submarines and surface combatants, plus contingency docking for future nuclear-powered submarines. Bechtel’s planning and feasibility studies will run through 2026, with the full precinct development anticipated over the coming decade, designed to meet evolving defence needs while minimising local disruption.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION GUIDES SOUTH FLORIDA'S GROWTH THROUGH COLLABORATION AND TECH

On the 28th of March 2026, during an industry roundtable hosted in partnership with the South Florida Business Journal, leaders from development, construction, and government agencies explored how South Florida’s skyline is evolving and the role DPR Construction is playing in guiding that change.Collaboration Reshaping Project Delivery: Participants stressed that cross sector partnerships have shifted from optional to essential for navigating modern urban development. By aligning stakeholders early, projects gain measurable improvements in efficiency, safety, and quality. In a market with limited land, rising costs, and increasing regulatory demands, integrated approaches are critical to delivering long term community value rather than just buildings.Innovation and Technology Driving Efficiency: DPR Construction and its partners are leveraging prefabrication, modular construction, and emerging technologies like artificial intelligence to streamline workflows, reduce waste, and shorten lead times. Digital tools and data driven decision making support better forecasting and coordination across complex, multi phase programs, while improving site safety and accuracy.Addressing Workforce and Growth Challenges: South Florida’s strong population and economy are translating into a growing need for skilled labor. Industry leaders highlighted partnerships with schools and training programs designed to attract, develop, and retain talent within the construction sector, ensuring a steady pipeline as investment accelerates.Building for Long-Term Resilience: Finally, the discussion centered on sustainable building practices, energy efficiency, and climate resilient infrastructure. Developers and contractors aim to design communities that endure large scale change, balancing speed, cost, and sustainability. DPR Construction is positioned at the forefront of this evolution, setting benchmarks for large scale urban development in high demand markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) WINS NYC DEP PROJECT FOR LARGE-DIAMETER STORAGE TUNNEL AND DEWATERING PUMP STATION AND SECURES USCG PROGRAM MANAGEMENT CONTRACT VIA GSI AMERICAS JV

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Posts Progress On Apr. 14, 2026 Contract Award To Lead The Construction Of The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel In New York City. The Project Features A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Curb Untreated DischargesThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Has Entrusted Parsons With This Critical Task, Citing The Firm's Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets, Improve Water Quality, And Support Urban Sustainability Through A Technology-Driven ApproachIn Addition, Parsons Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas That Secures A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Contract Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Significant Upgrades To Shore Infrastructure And Deployment Of Transformational TechnologiesThe Partnership Highlights Parsons' Strategic Intent To Assist Federal Agencies In Securing And Modernizing Critical Digital And Physical Assets. The Arrangement Aligns With The Company's Broader Focus On Defense And Cybersecurity Markets And Reflects Its Ability To Deliver Integrated Solutions For Urban And National Security ChallengesInvestors May Monitor The Company's Ongoing Growth Across Infrastructure And Defense Initiatives As Parsons Expands Its Portfolio

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AWARD WITH GSIA

Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) and its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) report a $25 million contract from the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services, granted on 15th April 2026, with a one-year base period and four additional one-year option periodsThe scope of the contract will support the USCG’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, a comprehensive modernization effort that includes upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure. The program carries a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and strengthening mission-critical operational capacitiesMartin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, stated, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." GSIA, for its part, will play a pivotal role in the joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, added, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture." Engineering News-Record has recognized Parsons as a provider of program management services, reinforcing the value of this collaborationThe partnership highlights Parsons’ capabilities in managing complex projects across master planning, design, construction management, and asset management, supporting national security and infrastructure initiatives

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) NAMED LEAD DESIGNER ON ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT IN $409 MILLION CONTRACT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been named the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a $409 million contract announced on 14th April 2026. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation awarded the construction phase to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, with Parsons responsible for the bridge design over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties. The project will span approximately two miles across one of the nation’s largest reservoirs, with 63 spans and substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface, under the Army Corps of Engineers oversight

urn:contentItem:6JGG-F693-RS9D-Y4V3-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ BECHTEL AND TERRAPOWER BEGIN CONSTRUCTION AT NATRIUM® KEMMERER UNIT 1

Today, Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC), serving as TerraPower's EPC partner, has begun mobilizing teams and transitioning into field execution at the Natrium® Kemmerer Unit 1 (KU1) site in Wyoming.

Source: Company Website

+ LUCY MARTIN NAMED PRESIDENT OF BECHTEL'S MINING & METALS BUSINESS

Bechtel today announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. She succeeds Ailie MacAdam, who will retire in May after an exceptional 41-year career with the company.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL SELECTED FOR PREPARATORY ENGINEERING ON OKLAHOMA ALUMINUM PROJECT

First new U.S. primary aluminum smelter in decades Bechtel has been selected by Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum to perform preparatory engineering and early execution planning for a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma - an investment expected to create thousands of jobs and support long-term economic growth in northeast Oklahoma.

When complete, the facility will be the first new primary aluminum smelter built in the U.S. since 1980 and is expected to double the current U.S. primary aluminum production, strengthening America's industrial base and supply chain resilience.

The project is expected to create approximately 1,000 permanent direct jobs and up to 4,000 construction jobs, delivering long-term workforce and community benefits across Rogers County and the greater region.

"Our team brings deep experience delivering complex U.S. megaprojects and aluminum facilities around the world," said Ailie MacAdam, President of Bechtel Mining & Metals. "We are proud to support EGA and Century as they advance this important investment in America's industrial future, and we look forward to working closely with our customers, partners, and the local community as the project moves into its next phase."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-HYN3-RSG5-X1WY-00000-00GNW : Ellomay Capital Announces the Filing of the Annual Report on Form 2F2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisADHOC-NEWSGNW : Ellomay Capital Announces the Filing of the Annual Report on Form 2F

for 2025

Tel-Aviv, Israel, , May 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Ellomay Capital Ltd. (NYSE American; TASE: ELLO) ("Ellomay" or the "Company"), a renewable energy and power generator and developer of renewable energy and power projects in Europe, USA and Israel, today announced the filing of its Annual Report on Form 2F for the year ended December 31, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

A copy of the Annual Report on Form 2F is available to be viewed and downloaded from the Investor Relations section of the Company's website at http://www.ellomay.com. The Company will provide a hard copy of the Annual Report on Form 2F, including the Company's complete audited financial statements, free of charge to its shareholders upon request.

The financial statements included in the Annual Report on Form 2F present certain changes compared to the unaudited financial results for the year ended and as of December 31, 2025 published by the Company on March 31, 2026. These changes include a decrease of approximately Euro1.5 million in project development costs mainly in connection with a reversal of provision due to a change in circumstances since the publication of the financial results, and a decrease of approximately Euro1.9 million in tax benefit for the year ended December 31, 2025.

About Ellomay Capital Ltd.Ellomay is an Israeli based company whose shares are registered with the NYSE American and with the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "ELLO". Since 2009, Ellomay focuses its business in the renewable energy and power sectors in Europe, USA and Israel. To date, Ellomay has evaluated numerous opportunities and invested significant funds in the renewable, clean energy and natural resources industries in Israel, Italy, Spain, the Netherlands and Texas, USA, including:

* *

* * Approximately335.9 MW of operating solar power plants in Spain (including a 300 MW solar plant in owned by Talasol, which is 51% owned by the Company) and 51% of approximately 38 MW of operating solar power plants in Italy;

* 16.875% indirect interest in Dorad Energy Ltd., which owns and operates one of Israel's largest private power plants with production capacity of approximately 850 MW;

* Groen Gas Goor B.V., Groen Gas Oude-Tonge B.V. and Groen Gas Gelderland B.V., project companies operating anaerobic digestion plants inthe Netherlands, with a green gas production capacity of approximately 3 million, 3.8 million and 9.5 million Nm3 per year, respectively;

* 83.333% of Ellomay Pumped Storage (2014) Ltd., which is involved in a project to construct a 156 MW pumped storage hydro power plant in the Manara Cliff, Israel;

* 51% of solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 160 MW that are under construction;

* Solar projects in Italy with an aggregate capacity of 210 MW that have reached "ready to build" status; and

* Solar projects in the Dallas Metropolitan area, Texas, USA with an aggregate capacity of approximately 38 MW that are connected to the grid, 11 MW that are currently in the test run phase prior to commercial operation and 14 MW that are under construction.

For more information about Ellomay, visit http://www.ellomay.com.

Contact: Kalia Rubenbach (Weintraub) CFO Tel: +972 (3) 797-1111 Email: kaliaw@ellomay.com

urn:contentItem:6JGB-XRV3-RRRH-604T-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - HIGHLIGHTS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEER COMPANIES2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - HIGHLIGHTS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEER COMPANIES

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ BECHTEL AND TERRAPOWER BEGIN CONSTRUCTION AT NATRIUM® KEMMERER UNIT 1

Today, Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC), serving as TerraPower's EPC partner, has begun mobilizing teams and transitioning into field execution at the Natrium® Kemmerer Unit 1 (KU1) site in Wyoming.

Source: Company Website

+ LUCY MARTIN NAMED PRESIDENT OF BECHTEL'S MINING & METALS BUSINESS

Bechtel today announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. She succeeds Ailie MacAdam, who will retire in May after an exceptional 41-year career with the company.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL SELECTED FOR PREPARATORY ENGINEERING ON OKLAHOMA ALUMINUM PROJECT

First new U.S. primary aluminum smelter in decades Bechtel has been selected by Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum to perform preparatory engineering and early execution planning for a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma - an investment expected to create thousands of jobs and support long-term economic growth in northeast Oklahoma.

When complete, the facility will be the first new primary aluminum smelter built in the U.S. since 1980 and is expected to double the current U.S. primary aluminum production, strengthening America's industrial base and supply chain resilience.

The project is expected to create approximately 1,000 permanent direct jobs and up to 4,000 construction jobs, delivering long-term workforce and community benefits across Rogers County and the greater region.

"Our team brings deep experience delivering complex U.S. megaprojects and aluminum facilities around the world," said Ailie MacAdam, President of Bechtel Mining & Metals. "We are proud to support EGA and Century as they advance this important investment in America's industrial future, and we look forward to working closely with our customers, partners, and the local community as the project moves into its next phase."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION COMMON STOCK [3 Press Releases]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabiliti

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabilities across cyber and electronic warfare, space and missile defense, transportation, water and environment, urban development, and critical infrastructure protection. Please visit Parsons.com and follow us on LinkedIn to learn how were making an impact.

Media Contact:Bernadette Miller+1 980.253.9781Bernadette.Miller@parsons.com

Investor Relations Contact:Dave Spille+ 1 703.775.6191Dave.Spille@parsons.us

Source: West Corporation

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider
