urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1MR-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ TRAVIS MROSS NAMED PRESIDENT OF ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION, AIMING TO ACCELERATE INFRASTRUCTURE FOCUS

Zachry Construction Corporation has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, a move intended to advance the company’s growth in transportation, water, and civil sitework markets.Mross joined in 2000 as an intern and rose to executive vice president, serving in that role for six years. Since his promotion on 21st April 2026, he will guide the company’s strategic direction and strengthen operations.In the new post, he will prioritize strategic growth and operational excellence across transportation, water, underground utilities, and civil sitework, while reinforcing a culture of innovation and results-driven delivery.The firm recently expanded its footprint with Crescent Constructors to bolster water and wastewater plant capabilities as demand for infrastructure grows. Zachry’s more than a century of experience underpins its safety practices, technical expertise, and reliable project management as it pursues future opportunities.

+ ZACHRY UNVEILS REBRANDING AS IT EXPANDS IN DATA CENTER, WATER MARKETS

Zachry Construction Corp. has refreshed its brand identity, updating its logo and adopting the tagline 'Unearth Opportunity' as it pursues growth in new markets. The changes were announced on Feb. 4, 2026, and are aimed at expanding the firm’s reach in data centers and water infrastructure while preserving its South Texas heritage.Executive Vice President Travis Mross said the rebrand reflects the company’s evolution, noting that the data-center market drives demand for sitework and underground utilities—from excavation to drainage.The company also strengthens its water-industry presence after acquiring Crescent Constructors Inc. in October, a move into the process and treatment side of water infrastructure. Mross called the water sector one of the fastest growing in construction and said Zachry intends to capitalize on that trend.Zachry has completed more than 5,500 projects for public and private clients. The firm’s history extends to H.B. Zachry Co., founded in 1924; after a 2008 reorganization, Zachry Construction and Zachry Group emerged as separate entities, with the branding update intended to communicate the mission clearly to clients and the public.

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED TO HEAD FEED IN LAKE CHARLES LOW-CARBON METHANOL VENTURE

Zachry Group has been selected to perform FEED activities for a landmark low-carbon blue methanol project in Lake Charles, Louisiana, a decision announced six days ago. The assignment covers the Front-End Engineering Design for the facility, which aims to capture roughly 1 million tpy of CO2 emissions and set a new standard in methanol production while advancing decarbonization goals. During FEED, engineers will finalize the technical and economic underpinnings, enabling a final investment decision (FID) in the second quarter of 2026. John Zachry, Chairman and CEO, said, "We are honoured to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project. This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale." The project aligns with the broader shift of the energy sector toward lower emissions, and Zachry Group's involvement signals a strategic focus on scalable, low-carbon technologies. Market participants and observers are tracking progress closely, as the FEED's success could inform future low-carbon ventures. With the FID targeting early 2026, Zachry Group could play a central role as the industry explores sustainable energy solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ ALBERT FAMILY FUNDS INDUSTRY-INTEGRATED CONSTRUCTION EDUCATION AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte is launching the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering through a leadership gift from Craig and Darla Albert. The effort, announced Apr. 7, 2026, aims to unite engineering, business and data science to cover the full lifecycle of capital projects. Rob Keynton, dean of the college, described the program as a cohesive framework designed to prepare graduates for rapid professional impact and strong industry partnerships. Lingguang Song will lead the new school as Executive Director, bringing a background in engineering technology and construction management. The Alberts’ initiative is the first phase of broader growth plans, including hands-on learning facilities, an integrated curriculum and endowed faculty positions, with the couple serving as co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign targeting $500 million for the university.

+ BECHTEL ELEVATES LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD MINING & METALS DIVISION

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam plans to retire in May after 41 years with the company. Bechtel President and COO Craig Albert remarked on 3rd April 2026 that Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision and sector experience to advance the Mining & Metals unit, noting Martin has spent more than 20 years at Bechtel building global teams to tackle complex challenges. Martin has said she aims to build on the division's momentum amid a shifting global market, emphasizing a focus on delivering results through cost-efficient development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes. MacAdam oversaw major projects in global markets and oversaw 2025’s strongest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals. Bechtel continues to serve Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and remains committed to outcomes amid an evolving energy landscape.

+ AUSTRALIA TAPS BECHTEL FOR HENDERSON PRECINCT MASTER PLANNING

Bechtel Infrastructure Australia has been named the design services consultant responsible for master-planning the Henderson Defence Precinct, a project designed to reinforce sovereign shipbuilding and sustainment in Western Australia. The appointment was announced on 9 March 2026, highlighting Bechtel’s readiness to drive the long-term development program.Funding for the precinct has included $127 million in 2024 to advance planning, with the Albanese Government later pledging about $12 billion to support the delivery phase. Bechtel’s role, supported by partners Architectus, Aurecon, WSP and H&B Defence, is seen as central to delivering these investments and supporting around 10,000 direct jobs over the next two decades.Key functions of the precinct include facilities for the Army’s landing craft, the Mogami-class frigates, conventional submarines, surface combatants and contingency docking for future conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarines. Planning and feasibility work will continue through 2026 as the project moves toward a decade-long establishment plan.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O&M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SHAPES RESILIENCE-DRIVEN URBAN RENEWAL IN SOUTH FLORIDA

On the 28th of March 2026, an industry roundtable hosted with the South Florida Business Journal gathered leaders from construction, development, and public-private sectors to discuss the rapid skyline transformation unfolding across the region and DPR Construction’s role in it.Collaboration and integrated project delivery were highlighted as essential in South Florida’s crowded, high stakes market. By aligning stakeholders early, teams can achieve faster schedules, safer sites, and higher quality, while navigating limited land and rising costs through more thoughtful, resilient design.Innovation and technology were identified as key accelerants. DPR Construction and partners are advancing prefabrication, modular approaches, and data driven planning to shorten timelines and improve accuracy. The emphasis on digital tools supports better forecasting and coordination across multi phase efforts, with safety and sustainability guiding decision making.With growth comes the need for a skilled workforce, educational partnerships, and robust training pipelines to sustain momentum. The conversation also underscored the importance of building climate resilient infrastructure and community centered design to ensure new developments endure.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) POSTS $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT WITH GSIA

Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) and its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) secure a $25 million US Coast Guard program management contract, awarded on 15th April 2026, with a one-year base term and four one-year optionsThe arrangement will support the Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, a major modernization effort for the Coast Guard that includes upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure and is backed by a $24.5 billion budget for modernization, training facilities, and enhanced mission capacitiesMartin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, remarked, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, said, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness and strengthens national maritime security." Parsons has been recognized by Engineering News-Record for its program management capabilities, reflecting the strength of this partnershipThe collaboration aligns Parsons’ project lifecycle expertise with GSIA’s energy and infrastructure capabilities to support national security and modernization goals

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DESIGNS ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT, SECURING $409 MILLION AWARD

The Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project is a $409 million undertaking that will be executed in two phases to maintain traffic. Parsons will lead the design while the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture handles construction. Funding includes a federal grant of $124 million awarded in 2024, complemented by state funding. Parsons stock has declined 35% over the past six months and currently trades at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America at Parsons, highlighted the company’s legacy in bridge design and its ability to deliver complex infrastructure solutions worldwide. In addition to Roosevelt, Parsons is advancing other initiatives, including a 10-year Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System contract with the Utah Department of Transportation, a $389 million I 64 Virginia design project, and project management consulting for Saudi Arabia’s Al Ittihad Sports Village. The stock recently received a Neutral rating from Baird due to concerns about potential revenue declines in the Middle East

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1M6-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ KRISTEN BEJARANO JOINS SWINERTON TO EXPAND MISSION CRITICAL AND DATA CENTER CAPABILITIES

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton has appointed Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, adding depth to its leadership in data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of experience delivering complex mission critical work for global technology firms. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and led Americas data center program strategy and procurement. In the new role she will lead the company's mission critical strategy, working with clients to translate technical requirements into plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Her background in cooling technology and renewable energy integration is expected to help Swinerton meet rising market demand. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, welcomed the appointment, highlighting the potential to strengthen client relationships and accelerate growth in the sector. Swinerton remains committed to quality and innovation and to serving projects across the United States as an employee-owned firm dating back to 1888.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRIO FOR NORTHEAST DIVISION

Swinerton has announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush as vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026, with Nicholas Schifferle appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive. The moves come as the firm intensifies its Northeast growth strategy.Quackenbush, a New Yorker, brings nearly three decades of experience leading complex construction projects and a strong grasp of New York City's building conditions and client networks that support both facility management and ground-up work.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, offers more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing design and construction programs across Asia and Australia and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. Murphy, who has overseen more than $650 million in commercial interiors projects, will lead multiple project teams to ensure high quality and client satisfaction, working with long-time division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana.Since its Northeast launch in 2021, the division has delivered a steady stream of office fit-outs, historic renovations and ground-up construction across all five boroughs and is expanding into education, residential and public sectors as the pipeline grows.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP CHANGE AS DON ADAIR STEPS DOWN AND CHRIS CHANY TAKES THE REINS

Swinerton announced that Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, after a nearly 40-year career in the construction industry. The retirement was communicated to employee-owners on June 13, 2025, as part of the leadership transition.Adair began his Swinerton career in 1988 as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles, later leading the expansion of the San Diego office before ascending to Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer in 2019.Chris Chany has been named Adair’s successor. Joining Swinerton Builders in 2013, Chany is known for fostering partnerships and driving strategic growth. He stated that he will maintain the company’s core values and focus on building relationships and delivering strong client experiences and dependable project delivery.The transition aims to sustain momentum for Swinerton in a competitive market and reinforce its standing as a leading player in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES GROWS PLATFORM WITH TRI-CITY GROUP DEAL, ADDS WILSON CONSTRUCTION CO. AND BILLINGS FLYING SERVICE

Quanta Services completed the Tri-City Group acquisition announced on 6th April 2026, expanding its integrated engineering and construction offerings for electric power and renewables.The Davenport-based Tri-City Group has a long history in the Quad Cities dating to 1895 and rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diversified services. It includes Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, with projects across 46 states and several countries.Lori Rochholz, Tri-City's marketing director, said the brand will remain unchanged and that the Palmer Family will continue to lead, ensuring continuity for customers and the community.Quanta CEO Duke Austin said the acquisition, together with Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service, broadens the crafts-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs nationwide. The company, which employs about 69,000 people, aims to meet growing infrastructure demand.

+ QUANTA EMPHASIZES DISCIPLINE AND PARTNERSHIPS IN 2026 OUTLOOK AT JEFFERIES CONFERENCE

Quanta Services outlined its 2026 strategy at the Jefferies conference on Mar. 3, 2026, signaling optimism after a solid prior year. The leadership stressed opportunities in clean-energy deployment and infrastructure, with plans to bolster operations and broaden offerings. A key focus remains the data-center market and risk-managed partnerships with midcap utilities such as NIPSCO to deliver tailored solutions. The company reaffirmed its commitment to organic growth and disciplined capital allocation, including selective acquisitions to create long-term value.

+ QUANTA SERVICES ALIGNS WITH UTILITIES TO DELIVER LARGE-SCALE ENERGY-TRANSITION PROJECTS ACROSS NORTH AMERICA

Quanta Services has become a central infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables and grid modernization, and its work underpins North America's energy transition. Not a consumer brand, its reach spans power lines, substations, pipelines and fiber networks essential for a more electrified future.The company focuses on engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, enabling utilities to outsource complex projects and shrink contractor-management hurdles, a critical capability as networks are upgraded and decarbonized.It operates through three core lines—electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure—allowing it to tackle high-voltage transmission, urban undergrounding and advanced communications networks, aided by LiDAR and grid digitalization.In a market with MasTec and MYR Group, Quanta differentiates through specialization and scale and its ability to deliver large-scale modernization, appealing to utilities investing in grid hardening and renewable integration; investors see a premium valuation indicating confidence in growth.

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ BARTON MALOW, SHERIDAN ANNOUNCE OUTREACH SESSION FOR NEW MACON ARENA

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers on the New Macon Arena project, aiming to outline the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities for SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE firms. The event is slated for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, 200 Coliseum Drive.Attendees will hear a brief presentation followed by a Q&A and time to network with project leads. Registration is optional but encouraged via the Barton Malow website.OSBA supports the outreach and stresses the importance of local participation; OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather notes that the size of a project does not come along often, and local businesses should have a competitive bid opportunity from the outset. Mayor Lester Miller adds that the arena is expected to be a significant economic driver for the city.The new Macon Arena will be constructed on the current Macon Coliseum site and is anticipated to cover over 230,000 square feet, with seating for about 8,500 for concerts. It will feature modern amenities and a flexible design suitable for various events, including sports, and Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction are collaborating to deliver a high-quality, community-focused project.

+ BARTON MALOW NAMES FOUR LEADERS TO GUIDE STRATEGY, INVESTMENTS AND PROJECT DELIVERY

On Mar. 6, 2026, Barton Malow announced a leadership repositioning aimed at accelerating growth and improving alignment between strategy and operations. Brandon Booth, formerly chief legal officer, was named chief strategy and risk officer to guide enterprise strategy and innovation while exploring external investment opportunities. Cesar Diaz, previously head of data and analytics, became senior vice president of strategy and investments to oversee strategic planning and disciplined capital deployment. Lindsey Rem was appointed senior vice president of innovation and project delivery, expanding collaboration with the education group to test new technologies and construction methods on active projects. Brandon Yezbick, the former CFO, now serves as chief financial and administrative officer, overseeing finance, human resources and technology initiatives. The leadership realignment underscores Barton Malow's focus on innovation and growth as it continues to pursue opportunities in a changing built environment.

+ BARTON MALOW NAMES LUKE KNOCHEL AS VICE PRESIDENT, PROJECT CONTROLS

Barton Malow Company has named Luke Knochel as vice president of project controls, effective Feb. 4, 2026. He will lead a multi-disciplinary team handling cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start-up, AP/AR processes, and change management. The appointment expands Knochel's responsibilities across multiple regions, with oversight of directors and managers as the firm grows its footprint in the construction sector. Since joining in 2013 as a cost analyst, Knochel has overseen project controls and finance on major projects, including the Little Caesars Arena, and has led Barton Malow’s Chicago, Columbus and Nashville offices. He previously served as project controls manager for the Midwest and later moved to a corporate finance role to manage the shared services group and implement a new accounts payable system. He will remain based at the Southfield headquarters.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SHAPES RESILIENCE-DRIVEN URBAN RENEWAL IN SOUTH FLORIDA

On the 28th of March 2026, an industry roundtable hosted with the South Florida Business Journal gathered leaders from construction, development, and public-private sectors to discuss the rapid skyline transformation unfolding across the region and DPR Construction’s role in it.Collaboration and integrated project delivery were highlighted as essential in South Florida’s crowded, high stakes market. By aligning stakeholders early, teams can achieve faster schedules, safer sites, and higher quality, while navigating limited land and rising costs through more thoughtful, resilient design.Innovation and technology were identified as key accelerants. DPR Construction and partners are advancing prefabrication, modular approaches, and data driven planning to shorten timelines and improve accuracy. The emphasis on digital tools supports better forecasting and coordination across multi phase efforts, with safety and sustainability guiding decision making.With growth comes the need for a skilled workforce, educational partnerships, and robust training pipelines to sustain momentum. The conversation also underscored the importance of building climate resilient infrastructure and community centered design to ensure new developments endure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WINS BID TO DELIVER WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT ADJACENT TO KIA CENTER

Turner Construction has been named builder for Westcourt Orlando, the 900,000 to 1,000,000-square-foot mixed-use district near the Kia Center, with enabling work underway as of 22nd April 2026.The project followed an extensive preconstruction phase. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, cited Turner's track record on complex builds, particularly in the Space Coast, as a decisive factor. "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us and build this project," Chapman remarked.Turner has operated in Central Florida since 1984, with an Orlando office and regional leadership under vice president Jeff Justen cited by developers as important factors in the decision.Westcourt includes a 265-key Kimpton hotel, about 269 high-rise residences, 300,000 square feet of Class A office space, 125,000 square feet of retail, a 3,500-capacity events venue, and roughly 1,140 parking stalls. Published timelines show a start in Q1 2026 and a potential finish by late 2028, though mobilization could slip to the end of 2026.

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition highlights PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace and strong employee engagement.It marks the 17th time PCL has earned a spot on the list, with a No. 91 ranking for 2026. The designation is linked to the company's employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Fortune's list is widely viewed as a gauge of organizational health and employee experience, often correlating with stronger business performance.In addition, PCL’s year also featured an Engineering News-Record ranking, placing No. 11 on the Top 400 Contractors list for 2025, underscoring leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while prioritizing people.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION LEADS INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS FOR COLORADO RIVER WATER SECURITY

On the 26th of March 2026, the Colorado River's drought conditions are tightening supplies across the West.Lake Powell is 162 feet below full and holds about 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a reassessment of basin-wide water management.PCL Construction is pushing for expanded water reuse infrastructure as a central resilience tool, aligning with a projected national average of $47.1 billion in water reuse investments over the next decade. Adoption varies among the seven basin states.With the fall 2026 expiration looming, PCL's Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix provides a model for maintaining reliable supplies amid growth, and participants at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference highlighted the need for a multifaceted approach.

The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. + CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

Both boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness and supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. + MCCARTHY BUILDS MOMENTUM IN HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION WITH CALIFORNIA TOWER MILESTONE

The California Tower milestone aligns with UC Davis Health's broader Vision 2030, a plan to more than double the campus footprint. Opening is targeted for 2030, finalizing a major expansion that will deliver increased patient capacity and upgraded facilities. In addition to this project, McCarthy recently completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December, underscoring the firm's active role in healthcare construction even as early 2026 market indicators hint at a potential slowdown in some healthcare-project planning.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS SETS INDUSTRY BENCHMARK WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS DISCLOSURE

McCarthy Holdings has released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, issued on 16th February 2026 and developed in collaboration with Gravity. The report maps Scope 1, Scope 2, and key Scope 3 emissions, with the Scope 1 and 2 data independently audited and given limited assurance, demonstrating a commitment to transparency in the construction sector.As an employee-owned firm, McCarthy emphasizes its core values and regulatory readiness, with sustainability anchored in three priorities: fully understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly toward people and communities, and efficiently managing energy use. The company notes that its emissions data inform broader practices, including biodiversity work and responsible fuel management, while its digital tools and AI support smarter project decisions.In addition, McCarthy highlights an advanced PV recycling program for solar panels, partnering with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and promote a circular economy. Through data-driven insights, the firm aims to strengthen communities and set a high standard for responsible construction.

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. + VPM AND CLARK CONSTRUCTION OPEN NEW HEADQUARTERS TO NEXT-GENERATION BUILDERS

VPM hosted students from the Metropolitan Business League at its new headquarters, which is under construction by Clark Construction. value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS

urn:contentItem:6JGF-SRG3-RTDN-M3YT-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY PULSE: KEY TRENDS AND PEER INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ BECHTEL AND TERRAPOWER BEGIN CONSTRUCTION AT NATRIUM® KEMMERER UNIT 1

Today, Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC), serving as TerraPower's EPC partner, has begun mobilizing teams and transitioning into field execution at the Natrium® Kemmerer Unit 1 (KU1) site in Wyoming.

Source: Company Website

+ LUCY MARTIN NAMED PRESIDENT OF BECHTEL'S MINING & METALS BUSINESS

Bechtel today announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. She succeeds Ailie MacAdam, who will retire in May after an exceptional 41-year career with the company.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL SELECTED FOR PREPARATORY ENGINEERING ON OKLAHOMA ALUMINUM PROJECT

First new U.S. primary aluminum smelter in decades Bechtel has been selected by Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum to perform preparatory engineering and early execution planning for a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma - an investment expected to create thousands of jobs and support long-term economic growth in northeast Oklahoma.

When complete, the facility will be the first new primary aluminum smelter built in the U.S. since 1980 and is expected to double the current U.S. primary aluminum production, strengthening America's industrial base and supply chain resilience.

The project is expected to create approximately 1,000 permanent direct jobs and up to 4,000 construction jobs, delivering long-term workforce and community benefits across Rogers County and the greater region.

"Our team brings deep experience delivering complex U.S. megaprojects and aluminum facilities around the world," said Ailie MacAdam, President of Bechtel Mining & Metals. "We are proud to support EGA and Century as they advance this important investment in America's industrial future, and we look forward to working closely with our customers, partners, and the local community as the project moves into its next phase."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

+ PARSONS CORPORATION COMMON STOCK [3 Press Releases]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabiliti

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabilities across cyber and electronic warfare, space and missile defense, transportation, water and environment, urban development, and critical infrastructure protection. Please visit Parsons.com and follow us on LinkedIn to learn how were making an impact.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.

urn:contentItem:6JG9-FS73-RRR8-C4RF-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATE: THE RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND OTHERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:15:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY UPDATE: THE RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

+ LUCY MARTIN NAMED PRESIDENT OF BECHTEL'S MINING & METALS BUSINESS

Bechtel today announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. She succeeds Ailie MacAdam, who will retire in May after an exceptional 41-year career with the company.

+ BECHTEL SELECTED FOR PREPARATORY ENGINEERING ON OKLAHOMA ALUMINUM PROJECT

First new U.S. primary aluminum smelter in decades Bechtel has been selected by Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum to perform preparatory engineering and early execution planning for a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma - an investment expected to create thousands of jobs and support long-term economic growth in northeast Oklahoma.

When complete, the facility will be the first new primary aluminum smelter built in the U.S. since 1980 and is expected to double the current U.S. primary aluminum production, strengthening America's industrial base and supply chain resilience.

The project is expected to create approximately 1,000 permanent direct jobs and up to 4,000 construction jobs, delivering long-term workforce and community benefits across Rogers County and the greater region.

"Our team brings deep experience delivering complex U.S. megaprojects and aluminum facilities around the world," said Ailie MacAdam, President of Bechtel Mining & Metals. "We are proud to support EGA and Century as they advance this important investment in America's industrial future, and we look forward to working closely with our customers, partners, and the local community as the project moves into its next phase."

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.
